disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cramping calf pain comes on reproducibly after walking about 50 metres and resolves completely with 5-10 minutes of rest
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly when the leg pain comes on and what makes it stop?
How far can you walk before it starts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes on like clockwork after about 50 metres — I have to stop, and after 5 to 10 minutes of standing still it goes completely, then I can start again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pain has been present for about 6 months and has sharply worsened over the past week, now triggered by shorter distances
trigger_question_examples: How long has this been going on and has it changed recently?
Is it getting better, worse, or staying the same?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been building for about six months, but this last week it's suddenly much worse — it now starts after a much shorter walk than before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pain in the leg or foot at rest, no night pain, no ulcers or sores, and the foot is not cold, pale or numb when resting
trigger_question_examples: Do you get any pain in your leg or foot when you are resting or lying in bed at night?
Has the foot ever gone suddenly cold, pale, numb or weak?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — when I sit or lie down there's no pain at all. My foot doesn't go cold or numb, and I've no sores on it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pain is relieved simply by standing still and resting, not by sitting down or bending forward
trigger_question_examples: To ease the pain, do you need to sit down or lean forward, or is standing still enough?
Does bending forward or sitting change anything?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I just have to stand still for a bit — I don't need to sit down or bend over, standing there is enough for it to settle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing yellowish raised patches on the eyelids and a greyish ring around the eyes; patient has avoided medical care for years
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any changes on your skin or eyes?
When did you last see a doctor or have any blood tests?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had these yellowish patches on my eyelids and a grey ring round my eyes for a long time. I haven't seen a doctor in years, so I've never had it looked at.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, no breathlessness, no facial droop, arm weakness or speech difficulty
trigger_question_examples: Any chest tightness, breathlessness, or dizziness?
Any sudden weakness, face drooping or trouble speaking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no chest pain, no trouble with my speech or my face, just the legs.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sedentary lifestyle with a mostly processed-food diet; lives alone with mobility now restricted by the leg pain
trigger_question_examples: Tell me a bit about your daily routine, activity and diet.
How are you managing at home with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't move around much and mostly eat packaged meals. I live on my own, and now the leg pain is making it hard to get out and manage.
